data.title: Anglo American writes down De Beers value by another $2.9B but analysts see progress
data.description:
Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) said Thursday it wrote down the value of its struggling De Beers diamond business by another $2.9B, after already writing down the business by $1.6B to $7.6B last year.

The latest writeoff contributed to Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) booking a total of $3.8B in net impairments for 2024, taking the company to a wider loss than analysts had expected.

Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) reported a net loss of $3.07B for the full year, far above the $116.9M loss forecast by analysts, according to a consensus compiled by Visible Alpha, after posting a net profit of $283M in 2023, while full-year underlying EBITDA, the company's preferred profit metric, fell to $8.46B from $9.96B, missing the $8.76B analyst consensus estimate.

The annual decline reflected a 10% drop in average prices of its commodities, Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) said, but noted cost cutting helped deliver $1.3B in savings with another $500M expected by the end of 2025.

But the market welcomed the company's progress towards asset sales and reduced debt, sending shares +4.6% in London trading.

Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) "reduced the book value of De Beers by $2.9B to bring the carrying value of this business to a more reasonable $4.1B," Jefferies analysts said, also praising the company's operational improvements and progress on restructuring.

The $2.9B De Beers writedown was in-line with expectations, according to RBC Capital's Ben Davis and Marina Calero, and the miner made a successful step forward by agreeing to a deal with Chilean state copper company Codelco to implement a joint mine plan between Los Bronces and Andina, which will deliver straightforward synergies.

data.title: Endava Non-GAAP EPS of £0.30, revenue of £195.6M; initiates Q3 and FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Endava press release (NYSE:DAVA): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of £0.30.
  • Revenue of £195.6M (+6.5% Y/Y).
  • Endava announces $100 million share repurchase program.
  • Net cash from operating activities was £32.0 million in Q2 FY2025, compared to net cash from operating activities of £35.0 million in the same period in the prior year.
  • Adjusted free cash flow was £31.6 million in Q2 FY2025, compared to £33.6 million in the same period in the prior year.
  • At December 31, 2024, Endava had cash and cash equivalents of £60.1 million, compared to £62.4 million at June 30, 2024.

  • OUTLOOK:

    Third Quarter Fiscal Year 2025:

    Endava expects revenue will be in the range of £198.0 million to £200.0 million, representing a constant currency revenue increase of between 13.0% and 14.0% on a year over year basis. Endava expects adjusted diluted EPS to be in the range of £0.31 to £0.32 per share.

    Full Fiscal Year 2025:

    Endava expects revenue will be in the range of £795.0 million to £800.0 million, representing a constant currency revenue increase of between 8.5% and 9.0% on a year over year basis. Endava expects adjusted diluted EPS to be in the range of £1.20 to £1.23 per share.

data.title: Shake Shack pops after showing margin improvement, issuing solid full-year guidance
data.description:
Shake Shack (NYSE:SHAK) rallied in early Thursday morning trading after delivering a strong Q4 earnings report that included revenue growth of 14.8% and same-Shak sales growth of 4.3%.

The restaurant company saw improvement with costs during the quarter. Food and paper costs fell to 28.0% of sales from 29.1% a year ago, and labor costs declined to 26.9% of sales from 28.5% a year ago. Occupancy and related expenses also fell from a year ago as a percentage of sales.

Restaurant-level profit margin improved 290 basis points from a year ago to land at 22.7% of Shack sales. EPS was reported at $0.26 vs. $0.25 consensus. Adjusted EBITDA came in at $46.7 million vs. $31.4 million a year ago.

The restaurant operator opened 43 new company-operated Shacks during the quarter and 33 new licensed Shacks.

On the guidance front, Shake Shack (NYSE:SHAK) sees Q1 revenue of $326.5 million to $330.9 million (midpoint $328.7 million) vs. $332.7 million consensus. However, the midpoint of the full-year revenue guidance range was ahead of consensus expectations, indicating confidence in the net three quarters.

Shares of Shake Shack were up 8.6% in premarket trading on Thursday to $120.75 The restaurant stock is in negative territory on a year-to-date basis. Short interest on SHAK stands at 9.7% of the total float.

data.title: Upbound says CEO Mitch Fadel to retire
data.description:
• Upbound (NASDAQ:UPBD) said its CEO, Mitch Fadel, has decided to retire from the role and from the board after 40 years with the firm.
  • The firm said its CFO and executive vice president, Fahmi Karam, will succeed Fadel as CEO.
  • The company has started an internal and external search to identify its next CFO.

data.title: Insmed beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Insmed press release (NASDAQ:INSM): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.32 beats by $2.49.
  • Revenue of $104.4M (+24.7% Y/Y) beats by $2.09M.

  • Balance Sheet, Financial Guidance, and Planned Investments

    • As of December 31, 2024, Insmed had cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities totaling approximately $1.4 billion.
    • Insmed continues to anticipate full-year 2025 global ARIKAYCE revenues in the range of $405 million to $425 million, representing between 11% and 17% year-over-year growth compared to 2024.

data.title: Luckin Coffee GAAP EPS of $0.12, revenue of $1.32B
data.description:
• Luckin Coffee press release (OTCPK:LKNCY): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.14
  • GAAP EPS of $0.12.
  • Revenue of $1.32B (+32.7% Y/Y).
  • Average monthly transacting customers were 77.8 million, representing a 24.5% year-over-year increase.
    • Revenues from self-operated stores were RMB7,233.8 million (US$991.2 million), representing a 41.7% year-over-year increase.
    • Same-store sales growth for self-operated stores was negative 3.4%, notably improved from negative 13.1% in the previous quarter, and compared to 13.5% in the same quarter of 2023.

data.title: Bandwidth reports mixed Q4 results; initiates Q1 and raises FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Bandwidth press release (NASDAQ:BAND): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.37 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $209.97M (+27.0% Y/Y) beats by $6.29M.

  • Financial Outlook

    Bandwidth is providing guidance for its first quarter and full year 2025 as follows (in millions) based on current indications for its business.

    For the full year 2025, Bandwidth's revenue guidance projects 8 percent to 11 percent year-over-year growth when adjusting for the expected cyclical reduction in political campaign messaging activity, which resulted in revenue in the first quarter and full year 2024 of approximately $8 million and $62 million, respectively.

    Full Year 2025

    Guidance

    Revenue

    $168 - $170 vs. $182.73M consensus

    $740 - $760 vs. $761.48M consensus

    Adjusted EBITDA

    $16 - $18

    $82 - $90
